generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components (Physical Acting Components)
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Drives the first axis via torque output (τ_M) in the MABI MAX 100 robot. Converts electrical energy to mechanical rotation."
]

instance GearWheelTransmission : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Reduces speed and increases torque between the motor and cycloidal gearbox in the first axis transmission chain."
]

instance CycloidalGearbox : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Provides high torque output (τ_out) with reduced speed for the first axis, contributing to motion accuracy."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted directly on the PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor providing real-time motor output angle (r_M)."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance SecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder mounted behind the CycloidalGearbox providing real-time system output angle (r_out) for backlash compensation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring input current (I_M) to the PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor for torque derivation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
]

instance Sinumerik840DController : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. CNC controller providing axis control inputs (position, speed, acceleration) derived from encoder data."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ControllerDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
]

instance MotorCurrentDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensor
]

instance ControllerDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom Sinumerik840DController
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLBlockDefinitionDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram (BDD) describing the structural composition of the first axis (motor, gearwheel, cycloidal gearbox)."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLToolingEnabler
]

instance SysMLInternalBlockDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram (IBD) modeling rotational/current flows between components in the first axis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLToolingEnabler
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink friction model implementing Coulomb-Viscous friction characteristics for the first axis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkToolingEnabler
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink friction model implementing Stribeck effect for dynamic friction behavior in the gearbox."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkToolingEnabler
]

instance TorqueDerivationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Data calculation model deriving motor torque (τ_M) from current (I_M) using manufacturer-provided dynamics parameters."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance IdealGearboxModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model simulating ideal gearbox behavior without friction for baseline comparison."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkToolingEnabler
]

instance MotorOutputSimulation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Component-specific simulation of PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor output characteristics."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkToolingEnabler
]

instance GearWheelSimulation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Component-specific simulation of GearWheelTransmission behavior under load."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkToolingEnabler
]

instance GearboxSimulation : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Component-specific simulation of CycloidalGearbox performance with friction compensation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkToolingEnabler
]

instance MotorEncoderData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time position data (r_M) from MotorEncoder transmitted via MQTT protocol."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance SecondaryEncoderData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time position data (r_out) from SecondaryEncoder transmitted via MQTT protocol."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance MotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time current measurement (I_M) from MotorCurrentSensor transmitted via MQTT protocol."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TorqueDerivationModel
]

instance ControllerData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Axis control inputs (position, speed, acceleration) from Sinumerik840DController transmitted via MQTT protocol."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ControllerDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance MotorTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived motor torque (τ_M) calculated from MotorCurrentData using TorqueDerivationModel."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TorqueDerivationModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CoulombViscousFrictionModel
]

instance AngularVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived angular velocity (ω_M) from MotorEncoderData and ControllerData for friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorEncoderData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CoulombViscousFrictionModel
]

instance FrictionModelOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated friction outputs (δr_M, δr_G, δr_out) from friction models for comparison with measured data."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CoulombViscousFrictionModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLToolingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML modeling tool enabling structural and behavioral modeling via Block Definition Diagrams and Internal Block Diagrams."
    DTDFVocab:enables SysMLModelingService
]

instance SimulinkToolingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink tool enabling executable simulation models for friction and system behavior."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionSimulationService
]

instance MQTTDataAcquisitionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol implementation for real-time data streaming from physical components to digital shadow."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataStreamingService
]

instance DigitalShadowReferenceModelEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital Shadow Reference Model structuring data traces, sources, and models for traceability."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataOrganizationService
]

instance AssetAdministrationShellEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Asset Administration Shell providing metadata management and traceability for all components."
    DTDFVocab:enables MetadataManagementService
]

instance DataProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Processing engine for data trace aggregation and abstraction from physical sensors."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataProcessingService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Unified dashboard integrating live data and simulated outputs for semantic understanding."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance SysMLModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Structural and behavioral modeling service using SysML for first axis components."
    DTDFVocab:provides StructuralModelInsight
]

instance FrictionSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Friction modeling service implementing white-box and black-box friction simulations."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionCharacterizationInsight
]

instance DataStreamingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time data streaming service from physical components via MQTT protocol."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeDataInsight
]

instance DataOrganizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Data trace organization service linking sources to models via DSRM."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataTraceabilityInsight
]

instance MetadataManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Metadata management service providing contextual information for all data traces."
    DTDFVocab:provides ContextualMetadataInsight
]

instance DataProcessingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Data processing service deriving torque, velocity, and acceleration from raw sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:provides DerivedDataInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Unified visualization service displaying real-time data and simulated friction outputs."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationFeedbackAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance StructuralModelInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structural and behavioral description of first axis components using SysML models."
]

instance FrictionCharacterizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time quantification of static, kinetic, and viscous friction within the gearbox."
]

instance RealTimeDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Live data traces (I_M, r_M, r_G, r_out) from physical components for analysis."
]

instance DataTraceabilityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Traceability links between data sources, processing components, and models."
]

instance ContextualMetadataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Metadata including source identification, timestamps, and system properties."
]

instance DerivedDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Derived physical quantities (torque, velocity, acceleration) from raw sensor data."
]

instance FrictionCompensationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adaptive control algorithm compensation for friction-induced deviations in motion."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceAlert : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Proactive maintenance alert triggered by deviations between simulated and measured data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance VisualizationFeedbackAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Operator feedback on friction-induced errors and correction recommendations."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C12: Environment
instance FirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Operational environment for the first axis of MABI MAX 100 robot with range [-90°, 90°] and max velocity 75°/s."
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor,
                  GearWheelTransmission,
                  CycloidalGearbox,
                  MotorEncoder,
                  SecondaryEncoder,
                  MotorCurrentSensor,
                  Sinumerik840DController
]

// C13: Components
instance GearboxFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Component representing friction dynamics in the CycloidalGearbox contributing up to 30% motion inaccuracies."
    base:isContainedIn FirstAxisEnvironment
]

instance MotionAccuracyComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Component analyzing positional accuracy deviations caused by friction in the first axis."
    base:isContainedIn FirstAxisEnvironment
]

// C14: System Under Study
instance FirstAxisSystem : DTDFVocab:SystemUnderStudy [
    base:desc "First axis of MABI MAX 100 industrial robot including motor, gear transmission, and cycloidal gearbox."
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment FirstAxisEnvironment
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The digital twin operates on a **real-time, continuous time scale** with dynamic updates synchronized to the physical system's operation. Key time-scale specifics include:
  - **Data acquisition frequency**: Not explicitly stated, but implied to be **high-frequency** (e.g., encoder readings and current measurements are streamed in real-time via MQTT).
  - **Model update rate**: Friction models are **continuously updated** during operation to account for changes in wear, load, temperature, or tooling.
  - **Visualization refresh rate**: Dashboard updates dynamically to reflect **live data and model outputs**, ensuring alignment with the physical system's state."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital twin constellation is organized as follows:
  - **Centralized Digital Shadow**: A single, purpose-driven digital shadow for the **first axis** of the MABI MAX 100 robot, incorporating:
    - **Data sources**: Physical sensors (encoders, current sensors) and virtual sources (simulation models).
    - **Processing components**: Simulation models (e.g., Simulation M, Simulation GW, Simulation GB) that consume input data traces and produce output data traces.
    - **Traceability links**: Each data trace is linked to its **origin** (e.g., sensor channel) and **processing logic** (e.g., friction model equations).
  - **Orchestration**:
    - **Purpose-driven**: The digital shadow is designed to **understand friction relations** and **detect friction loss** in the gearbox.
    - **Model-driven**: Combines **white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) friction models** for continuous identification.
    - **Dashboard integration**: Visualizes **real-time data** and **simulated outputs** to enable **semantic analysis** of physical relationships."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital twin covers the **operational phase** of the physical twin's lifecycle, focusing on **as-operated** representation. Key stages include:
  - **Design phase**: Not explicitly addressed; however, **engineering models** (SysML Block Definition Diagrams) describe the **as-designed** structure and behavior of the first axis.
  - **Manufactured phase**: Not directly modeled; the focus is on **real-time operational data** (e.g., wear, temperature, load variations).
  - **Operational phase**: The digital shadow is **continuously updated** during operation to reflect **real-world deviations** (e.g., friction changes) and improve motion control accuracy."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity considerations include:
  - **Model Fidelity**:
    - **White-box models**: Analytical friction models (e.g., Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck) with **known parameters** (e.g., load, temperature dependencies).
    - **Black-box models**: Data-driven models **eliminating parameter identification** for higher accuracy.
  - **Validation Mechanisms**:
    - **Comparison of simulated vs. measured data**: Deviations between reference values (r_M, r_G, r_out) and simulated outputs (ˆr_M, ˆr_G, ˆr_out) are analyzed to validate friction models.
    - **Traceability**: Each data point is linked to its **source** (e.g., sensor) and **processing model**, ensuring **end-to-end validity**.
  - **Uncertainty and Errors**:
    - **Backlash and positional uncertainty** are mitigated using **dual encoders** (motor and secondary encoder).
    - **Friction model errors** are addressed via **continuous identification** during operation to account for wear or environmental changes."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between the physical twin (PT) and digital twin (DT) includes:
  - **Data Protocols**:
    - **MQTT** for **real-time streaming** of time-series data (e.g., I_M, encoder angles) from the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. controller to the digital shadow.
  - **Network Architecture**:
    - **Centralized data acquisition**: Sensors and controllers communicate with the digital shadow via a **single data pipeline**.
  - **Data Formats**:
    - **Floating-point arrays** for torque (τ) and angular velocity (ω) in data traces.
    - **Metadata** (e.g., time stamps, source identifiers) embedded in each data trace."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The digital twin adheres to the following standards:
  - **SysML** (Systems Modeling Language) for **structural and behavioral modeling** of the physical system.
  - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** for **metadata management** and **interoperability** in industrial IoT contexts.
  - **MQTT** for **real-time data communication** in industrial automation.
  - **MATLAB Simulink** for **executable simulation models** (though not a formal standard, it is widely adopted in engineering simulations)."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The digital twin system includes the following security and safety considerations:
  - **Passive Operation**: The digital shadow operates in a **one-way data flow** (physical-to-virtual), with **no active control** exerted on the physical system, reducing safety risks.
  - **Data Integrity**: **Traceability links** ensure that all data points are **verifiable** and linked to their sources, mitigating data corruption risks.
  - **Safety Cell Integration**: The robot operates within a **restricted range [-90°, 90°]** and **maximum speed (75°/s)**, enforced by the **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. controller**, which acts as a safety barrier.
  - **No Remote Execution**: The digital twin does **not execute operations remotely** on the physical system, eliminating risks of accidental human exposure to unsafe conditions."
]
